name: Outlandish Oatmeal Bars
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (180°C). Grease a 13 x 9-inch (33 x 23 cm) baking pan with shortening. Place a cooling rack on the countertop.
2. In a large bowl, combine the shortening, brown sugar, egg, milk, and vanilla extract. Beat at medium speed of an electric mixer until well blended.
3. In another bowl, mix together oats, flour, baking soda, salt, and cinnamon.
4. Add the dry mixture to the creamed mixture in the first bowl at low speed, just until blended.
5. Spread half of the dough evenly in the bottom of the prepared pan. Spread raspberry preserves over the dough, stopping 1/4 inch (6 cm) from the sides. Mix white chocolate chips into the remaining dough and drop spoonfuls over the preserves. Spread evenly.
6. Bake at 350°F (180°C) for 30 to 35 minutes, or until golden brown. Do not overbake. Remove from oven and place on cooling rack. Run a spatula around the edge of the pan to loosen before cooling. Cool in the pan on the cooling rack. Cut into 2 x 1-1/2 inch bars.
Makes: About 3 dozen bars
